text:
- **Wiring**: `tenex-edge mcp` (`src/cli/mcp.rs:50`) runs a stdio JSON-RPC loop by default (`stdio()`, `src/cli/mcp.rs:57-91`) — this is spawned as a per-session subprocess by the harness (e.g. Claude Code). It's stateless itself.
- **Real state lives elsewhere**: every tool call is forwarded over a Unix domain socket to a separate, long-running per-machine daemon (`src/daemon/client.rs`), which auto-spawns if not already running. So the MCP process is just an RPC translator; sessions/channels/roster all live in the daemon.
